message:
feat(ndarray,conformance): add stride-tricks API (as_strided, broadcast_to, sliding_window_view) with full conformance coverage and reliability gate

fnp-ndarray: expand NdLayout with stride-tricks operations
- Add NdLayout::as_strided() to create arbitrary strided views with
  bounds checking against the base storage. The method computes
  required_view_nbytes from the requested shape/strides and rejects
  views that would read past the base allocation. Negative strides are
  explicitly rejected with a new ShapeError::NegativeStride variant.
- Add NdLayout::broadcast_to() which delegates to the new
  broadcast_strides() free function. This produces zero-stride axes for
  size-1 dimensions that need expansion, matching NumPy's broadcast_to
  semantics. Incompatible target shapes are caught via the existing
  broadcast_shape() validation plus an additional equality check against
  the merged shape.
- Add NdLayout::sliding_window_view() which constructs a higher-rank
  view whose output shape is [dim_i - window_i + 1, ..., window_i, ...]
  and strides are [base_strides..., base_strides...]. Invalid window
  dimensions (zero, or larger than the source axis) are rejected with
  ShapeError::InvalidWindowDimension.
- Add new ShapeError variants: RankMismatch, InvalidWindowDimension,
  NegativeStride, and OutOfBoundsView, each with descriptive Display
  formatting including the offending values.
- Add helper function required_view_nbytes() which computes the maximum
  byte offset reachable from a given shape/strides/item_size triple,
  used by as_strided for bounds validation.
- Add broadcast_strides() free function which maps source strides onto a
  target broadcast shape, inserting zero-strides for broadcast axes.
- Add comprehensive unit tests covering: broadcast_strides zero-stride
  insertion, rank mismatch rejection, incompatible target rejection,
  broadcast_to success and failure paths, as_strided in-bounds and
  out-of-bounds views, zero-extent views, negative stride rejection,
  rank mismatch in as_strided, sliding_window_view shape/stride
  construction, invalid window size rejection, and window rank mismatch.

fnp-conformance: extend shape/stride suite with stride-tricks fixture validation
- Add three new fixture cases to shape_stride_cases.json:
  "stride_tricks_views_success" (validates as_strided, broadcast_to, and
  sliding_window_view all succeed with expected shapes/strides),
  "stride_tricks_broadcast_expand" (validates broadcast_to with
  zero-stride expansion on a 3D tensor), and
  "stride_tricks_error_surface" (validates that as_strided rejects
  out-of-bounds views, broadcast_to rejects incompatible shapes, and
  sliding_window_view rejects zero-size windows — all via
  expected_error_contains matching).
- Add fixture data structures AsStridedFixtureCase,
  BroadcastToFixtureCase, and SlidingWindowFixtureCase with optional
  expected_shape, expected_strides, and expected_error_contains fields
  to support both success and expected-error validation paths.
- Extend run_shape_stride_suite() to construct an NdLayout from the
  computed base strides and then validate each optional stride-tricks
  sub-case. Success cases check shape and stride equality; error cases
  verify the error message contains the expected substring
  (case-insensitive). Missing base layout propagates as a test failure.
- Add as_strided_checked, broadcast_to_checked, and
  sliding_window_checked boolean fields to ShapeStrideLogEntry so JSONL
  logs record which stride-tricks operations were exercised per case.
- Extend the shape/stride log integration test to assert that at least
  one log entry exercises each of the three stride-tricks operations.
- Update fixtures/README.md to document the expanded scope of
  shape_stride_cases.json (now covers stride-tricks API checks).

fnp-conformance gate binary: add reliability infrastructure
- Add --retries, --flake-budget, --coverage-floor, and --report-path
  CLI arguments to run_workflow_scenario_gate. The gate now runs up to
  (1 + retries) attempts, stopping early on the first pass.
- Track per-attempt results in AttemptSummary structs recording attempt
  index, status, workflow log path, and suite summary.
- Compute a ReliabilitySummary with: number of retries configured,
  attempts actually run, count of flaky failures (the attempt index of
  the first pass), flake budget, coverage floor/ratio, and a
  diagnostics vector of ReliabilityDiagnostic entries. Three diagnostic
  reason codes are emitted: "deterministic_failure" (no attempt passed),
  "flake_budget_exceeded" (flaky failures > budget), and
  "coverage_floor_breach" (pass ratio < floor).
- The gate exits with code 2 on failure (unchanged) but now also writes
  the full JSON report to --report-path if specified.
- Add validate_workflow_log_coverage() which loads the workflow scenario
  corpus, parses the JSONL log, extracts scenario_id from each entry,
  and verifies full coverage (every corpus scenario has at least one log
  entry). Empty logs, missing scenario_ids, and partial coverage are
  all flagged as failures.
- Add derive_attempt_log_path() which appends ".attemptN" before the
  file extension for retry attempts (attempt 0 uses the original path).
- Extract parse_args() into a dedicated function returning GateOptions.
- Add WorkflowLogRequirementGuard (Drop-based RAII) that resets the
  log-required flag on scope exit.

workflow_scenarios: add log-required enforcement
- Add set_workflow_scenario_log_required() / workflow_scenario_log_required()
  using a new OnceLock<Mutex<bool>> static. When log-required mode is
  active and no log path is configured, maybe_append_workflow_log()
  returns an error instead of silently succeeding, ensuring the gate
  binary always produces observable log output.

Log append atomicity improvement (lib.rs, workflow_scenarios.rs)
- Refactor all four log-append functions (runtime_policy, shape_stride,
  dtype_promotion, workflow_scenario) to build a single byte buffer
  (JSON line + newline) and issue one write_all() call instead of two
  separate write_all() calls. This eliminates the possibility of a
  partial write leaving a log entry without its trailing newline.

scripts/e2e: wire reliability parameters into shell wrapper
- run_workflow_scenario_gate.sh now accepts REPORT_PATH as $2 (or
  FNP_WORKFLOW_RELIABILITY_REPORT env var), and reads RETRIES,
  FLAKE_BUDGET, COVERAGE_FLOOR from FNP_WORKFLOW_RETRIES,
  FNP_WORKFLOW_FLAKE_BUDGET, FNP_WORKFLOW_COVERAGE_FLOOR env vars
  (defaulting to 1, 0, 1.0 respectively).
- Invocation changed from bare "cargo run" to "rch exec -- cargo run"
  to run inside the repo container harness.
- All new parameters are echoed in the gate banner for observability.

Cargo.lock: dependency tree refresh
- Added: ahash, arc-swap, bumpalo, getrandom 0.3.4, js-sys,
  rustversion, wasm-bindgen (and macro support crates), web-time,
  zerocopy, zerocopy-derive.
- Removed: convert_case, crossterm, crossterm_winapi, derive_more,
  derive_more-impl, document-features, ftui-extras, ftui-i18n,
  ftui-runtime, litrs, mio, rustc_version, signal-hook, signal-hook-mio,
  wasi, winapi (and arch-specific sub-crates).
- Net effect: slimmer dependency tree after ftui subsystem removal,
  offset by new hashing/serialization transitive deps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
 This folder stores normalized oracle-vs-target fixtures for `fnp-conformance`.
 
 - `smoke_case.json`: minimal bootstrap fixture ensuring harness wiring works.
-- `shape_stride_cases.json`: broadcast legality + stride derivation checks.
+- `shape_stride_cases.json`: broadcast legality, stride derivation, and stride-tricks API checks (`as_strided`, `broadcast_to`, `sliding_window_view`).
 - `dtype_promotion_cases.json`: deterministic promotion-table checks.
 - `runtime_policy_cases.json`: strict/hardened fail-closed policy checks with structured log context.
 - `runtime_policy_adversarial_cases.json`: malformed/unknown wire-class inputs proving fail-closed behavior.

+REPORT_PATH="${2:-${FNP_WORKFLOW_RELIABILITY_REPORT:-$ROOT_DIR/artifacts/logs/workflow_scenario_reliability_${TS}.json}}"
+RETRIES="${FNP_WORKFLOW_RETRIES:-1}"
+FLAKE_BUDGET="${FNP_WORKFLOW_FLAKE_BUDGET:-0}"
+COVERAGE_FLOOR="${FNP_WORKFLOW_COVERAGE_FLOOR:-1.0}"

+echo "[workflow-scenario-gate] reliability_report=$REPORT_PATH"
+echo "[workflow-scenario-gate] retries=$RETRIES flake_budget=$FLAKE_BUDGET coverage_floor=$COVERAGE_FLOOR"

+  --report-path "$REPORT_PATH" \
+  --retries "$RETRIES" \
+  --flake-budget "$FLAKE_BUDGET" \
+  --coverage-floor "$COVERAGE_FLOOR"
 
 echo "[workflow-scenario-gate] completed"
